questionHtml:
Urea, (H2N)2CO, is excreted by mammals and can be used as a fertilizer.

The combustion of urea produces water, carbon dioxide and nitrogen.

Formulate a balanced equation for the reaction.
markschemeHtml:
2(H2N)2CO(s) + 3O2(g) → 4H2O(l) + 2CO2(g) + 2N2(g)

correct coefficients on LHS

correct coefficients on RHS

 

Accept (H2N)2CO(s) +  3 2 O2(g) → 2H2O(l) + CO2(g) + N2(g).

Accept any correct ratio.

[2 marks]

meta.title: The combustion of urea produces water, carbon dioxide and nitrogen. Formulate a balanced equation for the reaction.
breadcrumbLabels: 1.1 Introduction to the particulate nature of matter and chemical change
